label: Symbolic Order
description: The shared symbolic structure that organizes political identity and meaning.

label: Institutional Embodiment
description: The degree to which institutions carry and enforce the order.

label: Self-Preservation
description: The embodied order acts to preserve its continuity and authority.

label: Crisis / Sacralization
description: Crisis conditions intensify sacred claims around survival.

label: Correction Capacity
description: Pluralism, review, limits, dissent, memory, and institutional design restrain pathological escalation.

label: Symbolic Order Strength
description: Degree to which a symbolic order coherently organizes collective identity, legitimacy, obligation, memory, moral judgment, and sacrifice.

label: Sacred Enemy
description: Degree to which an opponent is represented as contaminating, existential, profaning, or morally absolute.

label: Pathology
description: Maladaptive self-preservation at the expense of truth, correction, personhood, and human flourishing.

label: Corrigibility
description: Capacity of an embodied order to receive criticism, acknowledge error, limit power, and repair harm.
